enriched_points.expanded_content: Biomimetic membranes replicate biological structures, like aquaporins, enhancing water transport efficiency, as seen in aquaporin-based biomimetic membranes (ABMs) that optimize natural filtration.

These membranes operate with significantly reduced energy requirements, achieving lower energy consumption in processes like desalination, making them more sustainable compared to traditional methods.

Biomimetic membranes exhibit superior selectivity, achieving water/salt permselectivities up to 108-fold greater than conventional membranes, enhancing overall filtration efficiency and effectiveness.
